title: Bridge Essay: Imageless imagery in aphantasia revealed by early visual cortex decoding.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through Imageless, imagery, in, aphantasia, revealed.

**Imageless imagery in aphantasia revealed by early visual cortex decoding.** — Curr Biol. PMID: 39798565. DOI: 10.1016/j.cub.2024.12.012. Activity in the early visual cortex is thought to tightly couple with conscious experience, including feedback-driven mental imagery. However, in aphantasia (acomplete lack of visual imagery), the state of mental imagery, what takes its place, or how any activity relates to qualia remains unknown. This study analyzed univariate (amplitude) and multivariate (decoding) blood-oxygen-level-dependent (BOLD) signals in primary visual cortex during imagery attempts. "Imagery" content could be decoded
